sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id Int32, arr Array(Int32), value String, created_at DateTime);
INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (1, [1, 2, 3], 'alpha', now()), (2, [2, 3, 3], 'beta', now()), (3, [], 'gamma', now()), (4, [1, 1, 2, 2], 'delta', now());
SELECT id, arrayUniq(arr) AS unique_count FROM test_data WHERE arrayUniq(arr) = (SELECT count() FROM test_data WHERE arr IS NOT NULL);

SELECT id, value, (SELECT arrayUniq(arr) FROM test_data WHERE id = outer.id) AS uniq_elements FROM test_data AS outer WHERE value = 'alpha' AND (SELECT arrayUniq(arr) FROM test_data WHERE arr = outer.arr) = outer.arr;
SELECT id, COUNT() FROM (SELECT id, arrayUniq(arr) AS unique_elem FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING unique_elem >= 1) AS grouped WHERE id = (SELECT max(id) FROM test_data) GROUP BY id;
SELECT id, value, arrayUniq(arr) AS unique_vals FROM test_data WHERE arrayUniq(arr) = arrayUniq([1, 2]) AND id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE value = 'beta') ORDER BY created_at DESC;
SELECT id, arrayUniq(arr) FROM test_data WHERE arr IS NOT NULL AND value = 'gamma' GROUP BY id HAVING arrayUniq(arr) > 0;
SELECT outer.id FROM test_data AS outer JOIN (SELECT id, arrayUniq(arr) AS unique_val FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING unique_val = 3) AS inner ON outer.id = inner.id WHERE arrayUniq(arr) <> 0;
SELECT id, arrayUniq(arr) FROM test_data WHERE (SELECT count(*) FROM test_data WHERE value = 'beta' AND arrayUniq(arr) = arrayUniq(['1', '2'])) > 0 GROUP BY id;
